name: Perkiomen School
about: Perkiomen School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school in Pennsburg, Pennsylvania, founded in 1875. It serves grades 6-12 plus a postgraduate year on a 185-acre campus in Montgomery County. The school offers more than 25 Advanced Placement courses and reports near-universal college placement.
college_matriculation: Graduates matriculate to a wide range of colleges and universities; the school reports approximately 99% college placement.
notable_alumni: John Cecil Holm (actor/playwright), Monte E. Ford (American Airlines CIO), Ryan Dunn (NBA player), Levi Stoudt (MLB pitcher)
financial_aid_notes: Approximately 50% of students receive financial aid; the school awarded more than $9 million in tuition assistance last year. Uses the Clarity Application with a February 1 deadline.

name: The Phelps School
about: The Phelps School is an independent boarding and day school for boys in grades 6-12 and a postgraduate year, located in Malvern, Pennsylvania, and founded in 1946 by Dr. Norman T. Phelps. The school emphasizes small classes and individualized instruction on a roughly 100-acre campus in Chester County. It maintains a low student-teacher ratio and offers ESL support for international students.
notable_alumni: Matthew Mellon (businessman), Kyle Vinales (professional basketball player), Mike Watkins (basketball player), Will Riley (basketball player)
financial_aid_notes: About 60% of students receive financial aid, with an average grant of approximately $7,000; merit scholarships are also offered.

name: Pine Forge Academy
about: Pine Forge Academy is a coeducational Seventh-day Adventist boarding school serving grades 9-12 in Pine Forge (Boyertown), Pennsylvania, founded in 1946. Historically African American, it provides a Christ-centered college-preparatory curriculum and reports that more than 98% of graduates attend college. The campus has historic Underground Railroad connections and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
college_matriculation: More than 98% of graduates attend college.
accreditation: Middle States Association (MSA-CESS), NCPSA, Accrediting Association of Seventh-day Adventist Schools (AAA)
notable_alumni: Barry C. Black (U.S. Senate Chaplain), Phife Dawg (A Tribe Called Quest), Clifton Davis (actor), Starletta DuPois (actress)

name: Solebury School
about: Solebury School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school in New Hope (Solebury Township), Pennsylvania, founded in 1925 on a 140-acre campus. It emphasizes interdisciplinary and experiential education with a low student-teacher ratio and small classes. The school enrolls roughly 250 students and reports 100% college placement in recent years.
college_matriculation: 100% college acceptance reported for 2022-2025 graduating classes.
notable_alumni: Margaret Mead (anthropologist), Andrew Bynum (NBA player), Shaun Cassidy (actor/producer), Elizabeth Pitcairn (violinist), Langhorne Slim (musician)
financial_aid_notes: About 45% of students receive financial aid, with an average grant of approximately $24,000.

name: Westtown School
about: Westtown School is a Quaker, coeducational college-preparatory day and boarding school in West Chester (Westtown), Pennsylvania, founded in 1799 by the Religious Society of Friends. It is the oldest continuously operating coeducational boarding school in the United States, set on a 600-acre campus with a lake, arboretum, and organic farm. The Upper School enrolls boarding and day students and reports 100% college placement.
college_matriculation: 100% college placement; graduates attend a broad range of selective colleges and universities.
notable_alumni: Mo Bamba (NBA player), Cam Reddish (NBA player), Holland Taylor (actress), Herb Pennock (Baseball Hall of Fame), Susan Eisenhower (author)
financial_aid_notes: About 52% of Upper School families receive financial aid (6.5% Lower School, 8.2% Middle School).

name: Wyoming Seminary
about: Wyoming Seminary is a coeducational college-preparatory day and boarding school in the Wyoming Valley of northeastern Pennsylvania, founded in 1844 and affiliated with the United Methodist Church while welcoming students of all backgrounds. The Upper School is located in Kingston and the Lower School in Forty Fort. It enrolls more than 100 international students from over 30 countries and offers 160+ courses including 24 AP classes.
college_matriculation: Graduates matriculate to a broad range of selective colleges and universities nationwide and internationally.
accreditation: Middle States Association (PAIS, TABS, NAIS member)
notable_alumni: Frank Carlucci (U.S. Secretary of Defense), Howard Gardner (psychologist), W.S. Merwin (U.S. Poet Laureate)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid available to both domestic and international students; contact the Director of Financial Aid for details.
